description: A plain screenshot of an online news article headline on a clean white background, with only the article publication's own typography (not an agency-designed graphic). In small blue uppercase eyebrow text it reads 'LACKAWANNA COUNTY,' followed by a large bold black headline 'Lackawanna College announces plans to open 3 new skilled trade centers,' a short horizontal rule, then a gray summary paragraph: 'Lackawanna College plans to open three new satellite centers in Bethlehem, Chambersburg, and Greensburg, focusing on skilled trades.' A faint light-blue decorative half-circle hatched graphic sits in the lower right as the outlet's branding. The agency reposted this as evidence supporting its 'Golden Age of the Skilled Trades' messaging, but the image itself is a media screenshot rather than a designed meme/poster. Target audience: supporters and the public, used as third-party validation of trades-growth claims.
notable_text: Lackawanna College announces plans to open 3 new skilled trade centers
